title: hecistopteris pumila
intro: Hecistopteris pumila, also known as the dwarf spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a small, terrestrial fern with delicate fronds and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Chinese and Ayurvedic practices, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. Hecistopteris pumila has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for its health benefits.
